1	"""Prompt-derived, pre-generation evidence scope contract.
2	
3	The contract partitions a copied evidence pool immediately before composition:
4	semantic OFF-topic sources are excluded, and definitively wrong-type/language
5	sources are excluded only when the prompt states an exclusive constraint.  All
6	excluded rows remain in the caller's corpus and are returned as disclosure
7	records.  Unknown classifications and judge failures fail open.
8	
9	This module has no retrieval client and no generator/verification dependency.
10	``deepen_scope_contract`` is the tested acquisition hook for retrieval runners;
11	prebuilt-corpus composers must not pretend they can deepen when they cannot
12	supply a real retrieval callable.
13	"""
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import copy
17	import re
18	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
19	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping, Sequence
20	
21	from src.polaris_graph.settings import resolve
22	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.exclusive_citation_eligibility import (
23	    known_non_journal_surface,
24	)
25	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.rq_eligibility import (
26	    DocumentType,
27	    _admitted_genres,
28	    _row_genre,
29	    _row_language,
30	    detect_language_offline,
31	)
32	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.saturation import marginal_novelty
33	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.topic_relevance_gate import (
34	    classify_topic_relevance,
35	)
36	
37	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled", ""})
38	_EXCLUSIVE_RE = re.compile(
39	    r"\b(?:only|exclusively|solely|restricted\s+to|limited\s+to|must\s+all\s+be)\b",
40	    re.IGNORECASE,
41	)
42	
43	
44	def scope_contract_enabled() -> bool:
45	    """Central default-ON composition contract flag."""
46	    return (resolve("PG_COMPOSITION_SCOPE_CONTRACT") or "").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
47	
48	
49	@dataclass
50	class ScopeContractResult:
51	    """One immutable-input partition plus complete exclusion telemetry."""
52	
53	    evidence: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
54	    kept_original_indices: list[int] = field(default_factory=list)
55	    off_topic_excluded: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
56	    wrong_type_excluded: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
57	    judge_failed_open: bool = False
58	    constraints: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
59	
60	    @property
61	    def excluded_count(self) -> int:
62	        return len(self.off_topic_excluded) + len(self.wrong_type_excluded)
63	
64	    def disclosure(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
65	        return {
66	            "input_count": len(self.evidence) + self.excluded_count,
67	            "composition_evidence_count": len(self.evidence),
68	            "off_topic_excluded_count": len(self.off_topic_excluded),
69	            "wrong_type_excluded_count": len(self.wrong_type_excluded),
70	            "judge_failed_open": self.judge_failed_open,
71	            "constraints": dict(self.constraints),
72	            "off_topic_excluded": self.off_topic_excluded,
73	            "wrong_type_excluded": self.wrong_type_excluded,
74	            "excluded_from_composition_only": True,
75	            "source_corpus_retained": True,
76	        }
77	
78	
79	def _hard_exclusive(prompt: str, constraints: Mapping[str, Any], key: str) -> bool:
80	    """Conservative hard-constraint detector; soft preferences never filter."""
81	    explicit = constraints.get(f"{key}_exclusive")
82	    if explicit is not None:
83	        return bool(explicit)
84	    values = [str(v).strip().lower() for v in (constraints.get(key) or []) if str(v).strip()]
85	    if not values:
86	        return False
87	    # Require exclusivity and a source-citation context in the same sentence.
88	    for clause in re.split(r"(?<=[.!?;])\s+", prompt or ""):
89	        low = clause.lower()
90	        if not _EXCLUSIVE_RE.search(low):
91	            continue
92	        if key == "source_types" and re.search(
93	            r"\b(?:source|citation|cite|article|paper|journal|report|book|preprint|website|news)\w*\b",
94	            low,
95	        ):
96	            return True
97	        if key == "languages" and re.search(
98	            r"\b(?:language|english|spanish|french|german|chinese|japanese|arabic|korean)\w*\b",
99	            low,
100	        ):
101	            return True
102	    return False
103	
104	
105	def _row_key(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
106	    return str(row.get("evidence_id") or row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "")
107	
108	
109	def _record(row: Mapping[str, Any], reason: str, category: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
110	    return {
111	        "evidence_id": row.get("evidence_id", ""),
112	        "title": row.get("title") or row.get("statement") or row.get("source_title") or "",
113	        "url": row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "",
114	        "reason": reason,
115	        "category": category,
116	        "excluded_from_composition": True,
117	        "retained_in_source_corpus": True,
118	    }
119	
120	
121	def _confident_language(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str | None:
122	    language = _row_language(row)
123	    if language:
124	        return language
125	    text = " ".join(str(row.get(k) or "") for k in ("title", "statement", "snippet", "abstract"))
126	    return detect_language_offline(text)
127	
128	
129	def _wrong_type_reason(
130	    row: Mapping[str, Any],
131	    source_types: list[str],
132	    languages: list[str],
133	) -> str:
134	    reasons: list[str] = []
135	    admitted = _admitted_genres(source_types)
136	    if admitted is not None:
137	        genre = _row_genre(row)
138	        if genre != DocumentType.UNKNOWN and genre not in admitted:
139	            reasons.append(
140	                f"source_type: genre={genre.value} not in "
141	                f"{sorted(item.value for item in admitted)}"
142	            )
143	        elif genre == DocumentType.UNKNOWN and set(source_types) <= {"journal_article", "peer_reviewed"}:
144	            # The salvaged publication-surface classifier is a conservative
145	            # negative proof.  No venue-name allowlist and no UNKNOWN exclusion.
146	            if known_non_journal_surface(row):
147	                reasons.append("source_type: known non-journal publication surface")
148	    if languages:
149	        language = _confident_language(row)
150	        if language is not None and language not in languages:
151	            reasons.append(f"language: row={language} not in {languages}")
152	    return "; ".join(reasons)
153	
154	
155	def apply_scope_contract(
156	    rows: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]],
157	    research_question: str,
158	    topic_judge: Callable[[str], str],
159	    *,
160	    constraints: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
161	) -> ScopeContractResult:
162	    """Return the citable composition pool without mutating ``rows``.
163	
164	    Topic judging is semantic and explicit-exclusion mode.  Any judge exception
165	    or malformed batch fails open inside ``classify_topic_relevance``.  Type and
166	    language exclusions require both a hard prompt marker and a definitive row
167	    mismatch; UNKNOWN stays in the pool.
168	    """
169	    copied = [copy.deepcopy(dict(row)) for row in rows]
170	    if not scope_contract_enabled():
171	        return ScopeContractResult(evidence=copied, kept_original_indices=list(range(len(copied))))
172	
173	    constraint_map = dict(constraints or {})
174	    try:
175	        topic = classify_topic_relevance(
176	            copied,
177	            research_question,
178	            topic_judge,
179	            # Ask the shared judge to retain/stamp its split verdicts, then
180	            # exclude only OFF_SUBJECT below.  The legacy hard-drop collapses
181	            # OFF_ASPECT into OFF and demonstrably thins topical breadth.
182	            exclude_offtopic=False,
183	        )
184	        topic_dropped = list(topic.off_subject_rows)
185	        dropped_ids = {id(row) for row in topic_dropped}
186	        topic_kept = [row for row in copied if id(row) not in dropped_ids]
187	        failed_open = bool(topic.n_failed_open_batches) or any(
188	            "fail-open" in note for note in topic.notes
189	        )
190	    except Exception:
191	        topic_kept, topic_dropped, failed_open = copied, [], True
192	
193	    dropped_ids = {id(row) for row in topic_dropped}
194	    original_index_by_id = {id(row): index for index, row in enumerate(copied)}
195	    off_topic = [
196	        _record(row, "semantic_topic_judge: off-topic to research question", "off_topic")
197	        for row in topic_dropped
198	    ]
199	
200	    source_types = [str(v).strip().lower() for v in constraint_map.get("source_types", []) if str(v).strip()]
201	    languages = [str(v).strip().lower()[:2] for v in constraint_map.get("languages", []) if str(v).strip()]
202	    if not _hard_exclusive(research_question, constraint_map, "source_types"):
203	        source_types = []
204	    if not _hard_exclusive(research_question, constraint_map, "languages"):
205	        languages = []
206	
207	    kept: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
208	    kept_indices: list[int] = []
209	    wrong_type: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
210	    for row in topic_kept:
211	        reason = _wrong_type_reason(row, source_types, languages)
212	        if reason:
213	            wrong_type.append(_record(row, reason, "wrong_type"))
214	            continue
215	        kept.append(row)
216	        kept_indices.append(original_index_by_id[id(row)])
217	
218	    # ``dropped_ids`` is deliberately computed to make the partition invariant
219	    # explicit and guard future refactors from accidentally re-admitting a row.
220	    assert not any(id(row) in dropped_ids for row in kept)
221	    return ScopeContractResult(
222	        evidence=kept,
223	        kept_original_indices=kept_indices,
224	        off_topic_excluded=off_topic,
225	        wrong_type_excluded=wrong_type,
226	        judge_failed_open=failed_open,
227	        constraints={
228	            **constraint_map,
229	            "source_types_exclusive": bool(source_types),
230	            "languages_exclusive": bool(languages),
231	        },
232	    )
233	
234	
235	def remap_finding_clusters(
236	    clusters: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]], kept_original_indices: Sequence[int]
237	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
238	    """Copy and remap index-based finding baskets to the scoped evidence list."""
239	    remap = {old: new for new, old in enumerate(kept_original_indices)}
240	    output: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
241	    for cluster in clusters:
242	        all_members = [int(i) for i in (cluster.get("member_indices") or [])]
243	        old_members = [index for index in all_members if index in remap]
244	        if not old_members:
245	            continue
246	        copied = copy.deepcopy(dict(cluster))
247	        copied["member_indices"] = [remap[i] for i in old_members]
248	        old_rep = int(cluster.get("representative_index", old_members[0]))
249	        copied["representative_index"] = remap[old_rep] if old_rep in remap else remap[old_members[0]]
250	        copied["corroboration_count"] = len(old_members)
251	        hosts = list(cluster.get("member_hosts") or [])
252	        if len(hosts) == len(all_members):
253	            copied["member_hosts"] = [
254	                host for index, host in zip(all_members, hosts) if index in remap
255	            ]
256	        output.append(copied)
257	    return output
258	
259	
260	def remap_same_work_groups(
261	    groups: Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]],
262	    kept_original_indices: Sequence[int],
263	    kept_evidence_ids: set[str],
264	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
265	    """Copy and prune same-work metadata so excluded members cannot re-enter."""
266	    remap = {old: new for new, old in enumerate(kept_original_indices)}
267	    output: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
268	    for group in groups:
269	        copied = copy.deepcopy(dict(group))
270	        member_ids = [
271	            str(item) for item in (group.get("member_evidence_ids") or [])
272	            if str(item) in kept_evidence_ids
273	        ]
274	        if not member_ids:
275	            continue
276	        copied["member_evidence_ids"] = member_ids
277	        canonical = group.get("canonical_index")
278	        if isinstance(canonical, int) and canonical in remap:
279	            copied["canonical_index"] = remap[canonical]
280	        elif isinstance(canonical, int):
281	            copied.pop("canonical_index", None)
282	        output.append(copied)
283	    return output
284	
285	
286	def deepen_scope_contract(
287	    result: ScopeContractResult,
288	    research_question: str,
289	    retrieve_fn: Callable[[list[str], Mapping[str, Any]], Sequence[Mapping[str, Any]]],
290	    query_fn: Callable[[str, Mapping[str, Any]], list[str]] | None,
291	    topic_judge: Callable[[str], str],
292	    *,
293	    target_count: int,
294	    novelty_floor: float,
295	    max_rounds: int,
296	) -> ScopeContractResult:
297	    """Acquisition-run hook: add eligible rows until target or saturation.
298	
299	    Every candidate is re-partitioned by both gates before it counts.  The hook
300	    never removes an already eligible row and never invents retrieval in a
301	    prebuilt-only caller.
302	    """
303	    current = result
304	    seen_rows: list[Mapping[str, Any]] = list(current.evidence)
305	    for _round in range(max(0, max_rounds)):
306	        if len(current.evidence) >= max(0, target_count):
307	            break
308	        queries = (query_fn or build_scope_deepening_queries)(
309	            research_question, current.constraints,
310	        )
311	        native_filters = {
312	            "source_types": list(current.constraints.get("source_types") or []),
313	            "languages": list(current.constraints.get("languages") or []),
314	            "is_retracted": False,
315	        }
316	        candidates = list(retrieve_fn(queries, native_filters) or [])
317	        if not candidates:
318	            break
319	        novelty = marginal_novelty(seen_rows, candidates)
320	        partition = apply_scope_contract(
321	            candidates, research_question, topic_judge, constraints=current.constraints,
322	        )
323	        known = {_row_key(row) for row in current.evidence}
324	        additions = [row for row in partition.evidence if _row_key(row) not in known]
325	        current.evidence.extend(additions)
326	        current.off_topic_excluded.extend(partition.off_topic_excluded)
327	        current.wrong_type_excluded.extend(partition.wrong_type_excluded)
328	        seen_rows.extend(candidates)
329	        if novelty < novelty_floor:
330	            break
331	    return current
332	
333	
334	def build_scope_deepening_queries(
335	    research_question: str,
336	    constraints: Mapping[str, Any],
337	) -> list[str]:
338	    """Prompt-derived additive queries for a retrieval-run deepening round.
339	
340	    Reuses the general decomposer and the evidence-type expansion transform.
341	    Desired source types come only from the extracted prompt constraints; no
342	    task, venue, or topic list is embedded here.
343	    """
344	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.evidence_type_query_expansion import (  # noqa: PLC0415
345	        expand_evidence_type_queries,
346	    )
347	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.query_decomposer import (  # noqa: PLC0415
348	        decompose_question,
349	    )
350	
351	    base = [research_question]
352	    base.extend(decompose_question(research_question))
353	    terms = [str(item).replace("_", " ") for item in constraints.get("source_types", [])]
354	    if not terms:
355	        return base
356	    return expand_evidence_type_queries(
357	        base,
358	        clinical=True,
359	        enabled=True,
360	        terms=terms,
361	    )
362

# (RCT / systematic review + meta-analysis / practice guideline). These qualifiers
# are appended to the anchor query so the search engines rank the primary

)

1	"""
2	Semantic topic-relevance gate — I-scope-001 (#1244).
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS (grounded diagnosis, do not re-derive):
5	A consolidated drb_72 breadth run cited 76 distinct sources with 0
6	fabrication, but ~9 were contamination — including 4 OFF-TOPIC-but-CREDIBLE
7	journals (spinal-cord stimulation, blockchain-sustainability, etc.). The
8	tier system rates CREDIBILITY, not RELEVANCE, so a credible-but-irrelevant
9	journal passes the tier gate. The lexical/embedding relevance floor ALSO
10	fails: en.wikipedia.org scored 0.583 relevance (above the clean median
11	0.500) because contaminants share generic content words with on-topic
12	sources. So neither tier nor embedding similarity can separate
13	off-topic-credible from on-topic — a TOPIC gate (semantic ON/OFF judgement
14	on the research-question domain) is required.
15	
16	This module is the pure, LLM-based topic gate. It is DEFAULT-OFF: the
17	orchestrator (run_honest_sweep_r3.py) only calls it when PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE
18	is truthy, and passes the production LLM callable + batch size. Keeping all
19	batching / parsing / exemption logic here (pure) means the gate is fully
20	unit-testable with a stub `llm_callable`, with NO OpenRouter key required.
21	
22	FAITHFULNESS LOCK: this gate is SELECTION-SIDE ONLY. It can only SUBTRACT a
23	candidate source from the pool handed to the generator; it NEVER edits a
24	sentence, span, or citation. strict_verify / the NLI entailment judge / the
25	4-role D8 audit / provenance are UNTOUCHED — every surviving sentence still
26	passes the identical faithfulness stack. Subtraction cannot fabricate.
27	
28	FAIL-OPEN CONTRACT (LAW II — never drop on uncertainty):
29	The gate drops a source ONLY on an explicit, confident OFF verdict. It KEEPS
30	the source (fail-open) on:
31	  - any LLM exception,
32	  - a returned verdict count that does not match the requested count,
33	  - any unparseable / unrecognised verdict line,
34	  - an empty / missing title+snippet (nothing to judge on).
35	A marquee / required-entity anchor is NEVER dropped, regardless of verdict.
36	"""
37	
38	from __future__ import annotations
39	from src.polaris_graph.settings import resolve
40	
41	import logging
42	import os
43	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
44	from typing import Any, Callable

46	_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)

48	# Default batch size for PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_BATCH — how many sources are
49	# classified per LLM call. Bounds cost: one call covers up to this many
50	# sources. A small denominator keeps the prompt short + the parse simple.
51	_DEFAULT_TOPIC_BATCH = 25
52	
53	# Cap on the title+snippet length fed per source so a batch prompt stays
54	# bounded even with long live-retriever statements.
55	_MAX_SNIPPET_CHARS = 320

58	def topic_gate_enabled() -> bool:
59	    """Kill-switch ``PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE`` (default ON — I-deepfix-001 DEFER-1).
60	
61	    Default flipped ``"0"->"1"`` (2026-06-30): the gate is the SEMANTIC
62	    discriminator the cite-surface off-topic suppression keys on (a lexical floor
63	    cannot separate on-topic from off-topic — disproven on drb_72). It is §-1.3
64	    keystone-compatible: the DEFAULT verdict is DEMOTE-not-DROP (every source stays
65	    in the pool, the confident-OFF ones carry the ``topic_offtopic_demoted``
66	    sidecar), so turning it ON adds a disclosed WEIGHT, never a hard filter. Set
67	    ``PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE=0`` to restore the byte-identical legacy (gate never
68	    called) behaviour."""
69	    raw = resolve('PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE').strip().lower()
70	    return raw not in ("0", "false", "no", "off", "")
71

73	def resume_run_topic_judge_enabled() -> bool:
74	    """Kill-switch ``PG_RESUME_RUN_TOPIC_JUDGE`` (default OFF — I-deepfix-001 wave-2).
75	
76	    A RESUME normally SKIPS this gate (the orchestrator guards the call with
77	    ``not _resume_active`` because the corpus_snapshot is reloaded post-selection). That
78	    leaves every reloaded row UNJUDGED, so an off-topic source can leak into the finding
79	    surface. When this flag is set, the orchestrator ALSO runs the EXISTING
80	    :func:`classify_topic_relevance` on a resume, so the reloaded rows get a topic verdict
81	    stamped. Default OFF => the ``not _resume_active`` short-circuit holds => byte-identical
82	    (the judge is not run on a resume, exactly as before)."""
83	    raw = resolve('PG_RESUME_RUN_TOPIC_JUDGE').strip().lower()
84	    return raw in ("1", "true", "on", "yes", "enabled")
85

87	def mark_topic_judge_ran() -> None:
88	    """Set the run-scoped signal ``PG_TOPIC_JUDGE_RAN=1`` the moment the topic judge has
89	    executed this run (fresh or the resume path opened by
90	    :func:`resume_run_topic_judge_enabled`). The downstream unjudged-topic quarantine
91	    (``weighted_enrichment.topic_judge_ran`` / ``partition_unjudged_topic_rows``) reads this
92	    to prove the judge ran — so a row that STILL lacks a verdict is a genuine leak
93	    (quarantinable), NEVER a legitimately-skipped-judge false positive. Process/run-scoped
94	    (each sweep is its own process); the quarantine ALSO derives the same fact from the data,
95	    so this write is the explicit belt to that suspenders."""
96	    os.environ["PG_TOPIC_JUDGE_RAN"] = "1"

99	def topic_gate_hard_drop_enabled() -> bool:
100	    """LEGACY escape hatch ``PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE_HARD_DROP`` (default OFF).
101	
102	    §-1.3 (WEIGHT, DON'T FILTER — the operator names the "scope hard-filter" as a
103	    BANNED anti-pattern; the ONLY hard gate is the faithfulness engine): a
104	    confident-OFF source is, by DEFAULT, KEPT in the pool and DEMOTED (disclosed
105	    as off-topic), NEVER hard-dropped. Topicality is a WEIGHT, not a DROP — the
106	    source still flows to composition where the UNCHANGED strict_verify / NLI /
107	    4-role faithfulness engine is the only gate. Set this flag truthy ONLY to
108	    restore the pre-I-deepfix-001 hard-drop (audit / reversal); the default
109	    keep-all + demote is the §-1.3-correct behavior."""
110	    raw = resolve('PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE_HARD_DROP').strip().lower()
111	    return raw not in ("0", "false", "no", "off", "")

114	def topic_gate_subject_aspect_split_enabled() -> bool:
115	    """Flag ``PG_TOPIC_GATE_SUBJECT_ASPECT_SPLIT`` (default ON — I-deepfix-003 #1374 Fix 3).
116	
117	    Splits the single confident-OFF verdict into two kinds:
118	      - ``OFF_ASPECT`` — the SAME subject entity but a DIFFERENT aspect / use-case /
119	        population than the question asks about (a topic-adjacent hub, e.g. an
120	        education-AI paper for a labor-market question). DEMOTE-and-keep, NEVER deletable.
121	      - ``OFF_SUBJECT`` — a CLEARLY DIFFERENT subject entity (different field / domain —
122	        scholar-mill / unrelated-domain junk). This is the ONLY deletable OFF: it alone
123	        carries the ``topic_off_subject=True`` sidecar the downstream junk-deletion gate
124	        keys on.
125	    A legacy plain ``OFF`` parses as OFF_ASPECT (conservative — never delete on the old
126	    verdict form). Set ``PG_TOPIC_GATE_SUBJECT_ASPECT_SPLIT=0`` to restore the
127	    byte-identical legacy ON/OFF prompt + parser (no ``topic_off_subject`` stamp)."""
128	    raw = resolve('PG_TOPIC_GATE_SUBJECT_ASPECT_SPLIT').strip().lower()
129	    return raw not in ("0", "false", "no", "off", "")

132	def junk_chrome_before_offtopic_enabled() -> bool:
133	    """Flag ``PG_JUNK_CHROME_BEFORE_OFFTOPIC`` (default ON — I-deepfix-003 #1374 Fix 4).
134	
135	    A chrome non-source (bot/captcha/cookie/404/login/empty — a FAILED FETCH, not a source)
136	    has nothing real to judge for topicality. When ON, such a row is NOT sent to the topic
137	    judge at all: it is KEPT (flows to the downstream content-integrity stamp + chrome-delete
138	    leg) and is NEVER stamped ``topic_off_subject`` / ``topic_offtopic_demoted``, so a garbled
139	    body + a chrome title can never be mislabeled ``confirmed_offtopic`` (Cause 3). OFF =>
140	    byte-identical legacy (every non-exempt row with judgeable text is judged)."""
141	    raw = resolve('PG_JUNK_CHROME_BEFORE_OFFTOPIC').strip().lower()
142	    return raw not in ("0", "false", "no", "off", "")

145	# Content fields scanned (widest-first) for the chrome detector — mirrors the
146	# run_honest_sweep_r3 content-integrity stamp pass so the two screens judge the SAME body.
147	_CHROME_BODY_FIELDS = (
148	    "fetched_body", "full_text", "content", "extracted_text", "raw_content",
149	    "raw_text", "page_text", "direct_quote", "statement", "source_text", "body", "text",
150	)

152	
153	def _row_is_chrome_nonsource(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
154	    """True iff the CONTENT-INTEGRITY detector confirms this row is a chrome non-source
155	    (bot/captcha/cookie/404/login/empty). Reads a pre-existing ``content_integrity_junk``
156	    stamp first (cheap), else runs the pure leaf detector on the WIDEST body + url + title
157	    (the long-body Zyte-recovery guard inside the detector protects a real source whose title
158	    is a stale bot page). FAIL-OPEN: any import / error / non-dict => False (row is judged
159	    normally — a detector bug must never silently skip a real source)."""
160	    try:
161	        if not isinstance(row, dict):
162	            return False
163	        v = row.get("content_integrity_junk")
164	        if bool(v) and str(v).strip().lower() not in ("0", "false", "no", "off", ""):
165	            return True
166	        from src.tools.access_bypass import (  # noqa: PLC0415
167	            detect_content_integrity_junk as _detect_ci_junk,
168	        )
169	        body = max(
170	            (str(row.get(_bk) or "") for _bk in _CHROME_BODY_FIELDS),
171	            key=len, default="",
172	        )
173	        url = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "")
174	        title = str(row.get("title") or row.get("source_title") or row.get("statement") or "")
175	        is_junk, _cls = _detect_ci_junk(body, url, title)
176	        return bool(is_junk)
177	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a detector defect must never skip a real source
178	        return False
179	
180	
181	def topic_batch_size() -> int:
182	    """``PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_BATCH`` (default 25), the max sources per LLM call.
183	    A non-positive / unparseable value falls back to the default (FAIL-SAFE:
184	    a garbage batch size must never produce a zero-size loop)."""
185	    raw = resolve('PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_BATCH').strip()
186	    if not raw:
187	        return _DEFAULT_TOPIC_BATCH
188	    try:
189	        value = int(raw)
190	    except ValueError:
191	        return _DEFAULT_TOPIC_BATCH
192	    return value if value > 0 else _DEFAULT_TOPIC_BATCH

209	    # ``kept_rows`` (the source flows to composition) but are disclosed here as
210	    # off-topic-demoted. Empty when the legacy hard-drop flag is set.

222	def _row_title_text(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
223	    """Title-like text accessor mirroring evidence_selector._row_title_text.
224	
225	    Live evidence rows populate ``statement`` with ``cand.title[:300]`` (not
226	    ``title``). Precedence: explicit ``title`` > ``statement`` >
227	    ``source_title`` > "". Returns a plain string (never None)."""
228	    for key in ("title", "statement", "source_title"):
229	        v = row.get(key)
230	        if isinstance(v, str) and v:
231	            return v
232	    return ""

236	    """Short snippet for the topic judgement. Uses ``snippet`` /
237	    ``direct_quote`` (whichever is present), bounded to _MAX_SNIPPET_CHARS."""
238	    for key in ("snippet", "direct_quote", "summary"):
239	        v = row.get(key)

245	def _row_is_marquee_anchor(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
246	    """True iff the row is a marquee / required-entity anchor that must NOT be
247	    dropped. Mirrors evidence_selector._row_is_marquee_anchor (I-pipe-006
248	    #1231) — a truthy ``is_marquee`` / ``required_entity`` / ``anchor_seed`` /
249	    ``is_anchor`` / ``entity_anchor`` / ``marquee`` flag, OR a
250	    ``required_entity``/``anchor`` substring in ``seed_source`` /
251	    ``query_origin`` / ``seed_query_origin``."""
252	    if not isinstance(row, dict):
253	        return False
254	    for flag in ("is_marquee", "required_entity", "anchor_seed", "is_anchor",
255	                 "entity_anchor", "marquee"):
256	        if row.get(flag):
257	            return True
258	    seed_source = str(row.get("seed_source") or "").lower()
259	    if "required_entity" in seed_source or "anchor" in seed_source:
260	        return True
261	    for origin_key in ("query_origin", "seed_query_origin"):
262	        origin = str(row.get(origin_key) or "").lower()
263	        if "required_entity" in origin or "anchor" in origin:
264	            return True
265	    return False
